title: afrocarpus gracilior
intro: Afrocarpus gracilior, also known as the East African yellow-wood tree, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the coastal regions of East Africa. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its bark, leaves, and seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Afrocarpus gracilior include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. Afrocarpus gracilior is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, to treat a range of ailments.
